title: files_modified/issues_worked stored as newline-joined TEXT blobs — cannot be queried or indexed
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Architectural Alignment (2) / Performance & Complexity (6)

Location: src/memory.rs:672-690 save_session_summary (joins Vec<String> with '\n'), lines 715-738 get_recent_sessions (splits on '\n' to rebuild Vec<String>).

Violation: Session summaries serialize files_modified: &[String] and issues_worked: &[String] by joining with newlines and storing in a TEXT column (v2 schema, line 273). Problems:

1. Cannot query: 'which sessions touched src/foo.rs?' requires LIKE '%src/foo.rs%' — table scan, no index, false positives for files whose paths contain another file's path as substring.
2. No escaping: a file path containing a literal newline (valid on Unix) corrupts the serialization and the split round-trip returns the wrong Vec. Unlikely but reachable via malicious tool arg.
3. Breaks first normal form. The intent is a many-to-many (session, file) and (session, issue) relationship — there is already a recent_activity table that represents this correctly. save_session_summary just denormalizes redundantly.
4. Round-trip brittleness: an entry containing \r\n on Windows splits differently on the reader side.

Mandated Refactor:
1. Delete the files_modified and issues_worked TEXT columns from recent_sessions. Query the existing recent_activity table on demand via get_session_files_modified (already exists at line 807) and get_session_issues (line 829).
2. Add a view: CREATE VIEW session_with_files AS SELECT rs.*, group_concat(ra.target, CHAR(10)) AS files_modified FROM recent_sessions rs LEFT JOIN recent_activity ra ON ra.session_id = rs.session_id AND ra.activity_type IN ('file_write','file_edit') GROUP BY rs.session_id — lets legacy callers keep the string API while using the normalized storage.
3. Migration v4: backfill recent_activity from the TEXT blobs on upgrade, then drop the columns.
4. get_recent_sessions now JOINs instead of splitting strings.
